patch.value: Intentional package CommandError outcomes create high-severity Sentry issues (PM-CLI-16)

Typed package/extension CommandError values with an explicit numeric exitCode represent handled command outcomes, but sentryCaptureCliError currently records generic-failure exit code 1 as an exception. Preserve raw Error capture while treating explicit CommandError control flow like PmCliError so telemetry retains the failed command without creating unresolved error groups. Duplicate check: exact PM-CLI-16 and verify-release title searches across all statuses found only historical evidence comments, not a canonical active defect item.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: pm search PM-CLI-16 --status all and exact verify-release title search found historical comments only. Live Sentry PM-CLI-16 is handled=yes, isUnhandled=false, CommandError exitCode=1, last seen 2026-07-13T21:38Z on release 2026.7.13; raw extension failures must remain captured.

author: codex-root
message: Treat explicit package CommandError outcomes as handled telemetry

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Implementation evidence: Sentry capture now suppresses only typed CommandError instances with numeric exit codes representing handled command outcomes; raw/untyped failures remain captured. Dedicated regressions and all exact-tree strict gates pass at 100/100/100/100 coverage.

author: codex-gpt5

patch.value: Sentry capture skips only Error values named CommandError with an explicit numeric exitCode; raw Error and untyped CommandError values remain captured; command-finish telemetry retains the nonzero outcome; focused regressions and the live Sentry gate pass.
pm-cli 2026.7.13; Node v26.4.0; Linux; live handled Sentry PM-CLI-16 exitCode=1
Run a package command that intentionally returns CommandError with numeric exitCode 1, such as a verify-release validation failure. Before the fix sentryCaptureCliError records it as an exception.
Typed handled CommandError outcomes remain in command telemetry without creating unresolved error-level Sentry groups.
The previous capture helper recognized PmCliError but captured extension/package CommandError outcomes as generic exceptions.
Sentry CLI error classification and package runtime
Expected package validation outcomes polluted Sentry and could block daily releases.
